revision.text:
[[fr:Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gu]][[ja:鳥海月山両所宮]][[simple:Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gu]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=File:Chōkai Gassan Ryōshogū torii - 2012.jpg |deity=[[Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto]]
[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] |Shrine |name=Chōkai Gassan Ryōshogū (鳥海月山両所宮) |location={{ill|Yamagata (city)|en|Yamagata (city)|qid=Q205526|lt=Yamagata City|1=Yamagata (city)}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|qid=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}, Japan |address=Miyamachi 3-8-41 |coordinates={{Coord|38.270928|N|140.337183|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3080728}}
{{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |consecration_year=6th year of Kōhei (康平) (1063) |type=Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}; {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} |Main Festival=August 1 |Rituals=Kokudameshi |Map={{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=16|lat_sec=15.34|lon_deg=140|lon_min=20|lon_sec=13.86|label=Chōkai Gassan Ryōshogū|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=|qq=}} |prefecture={{ill|Yamagata|qid=Q205526|1=Yamagata (city)}} }} Chokai Gassan Ryoshogu[http://yamagata-jinjyacho.or.jp/wp/blog/archives/jinjya/%E9%B3%A5%E6%B5%B7%E6%9C%88%E5%B1%B1%E4%B8%A1%E6%89%80%E5%AE%AE 山形県神社庁による紹介] (鳥海月山両所宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Yamagata|en|Yamagata|qid=Q230637|lt=Yamagata City|1=Yamagata}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|qid=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}. It used to be a prefectural shrine. Now it is a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. It is also called Kichigotomiya and Takemon Kichitomiya. It has a festival every July 1 of the lunar calendar, rice, cucumbers, eggplants, millet, and ears of {{ill|Decomposition|en|Decomposition|qid=Q339062|lt=rice|1=Decomposition}} are wrapped in paper and buried in the ground {{Cite web|date=2021-10-30|title=「腐敗」を確認、豊凶どっち 山形・鳥海月山両所宮で「穀様し」|url=https://kahoku.news/articles/20211029khn000040.html|access-date=2021-10-30|website=河北新報オンラインニュース|language=ja|qq=}} The name of the shrine in {{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|qid=Q82772|lt=Kanji|1=Kanji}} is Chōkai Gassan Ryōshō-gū (鳥海月山両所宮). This is what the parts of its name mean. Chōkai (鳥海) is the name of the nearby {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|qid=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai|1=Mount Chōkai}}, while Gassan (月山) is the name of the nearby {{ill|Mount Gassan|en|Mount Gassan|qid=Q3174863|lt=Mount Gassan|1=Mount Gassan}}. Ryōshō (両所) means "two places", and gū (宮) means "shrine". This means that the name can be translated to "The Shrine of Two Places, Mount Chōkai and Mount Gassan". The name reflects the shrine's importance in the local Shinto tradition, as it is dedicated to the kami (gods) believed to protect both Mount Chōkai and Mount Gassan. It enshrines the deities [[Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto]] of [[Ōmonoimi-jinja]] ({{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|qid=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai|1=Mount Chōkai}}) and [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] of [[Gassan Shrine]] ({{ill|Mount Gassan|en|Mount Gassan|qid=Q3174863|lt=Mount Gassan|1=Mount Gassan}}). ==Overview== '''Chōkai Gassan Ryōshogū''' (鳥海月山両所宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yamagata (city)|en|Yamagata (city)|qid=Q205526|lt=Yamagata City|1=Yamagata (city)}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|qid=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} and now a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|qid=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is also known as '''Kichijō Shrine''' or '''Bumon Kichijō Shrine'''. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3080728}}, the deity of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|qid=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai|1=Mount Chōkai}}, and {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, the deity of {{ill|Mount Gassan|en|Mount Gassan|qid=Q3174863|lt=Mount Gassan|1=Mount Gassan}}, part of the {{ill|Three Mountains of Dewa|en|Three Mountains of Dewa|qid=Q1155726|lt=Three Mountains of Dewa|1=Three Mountains of Dewa}}. == History == According to shrine legend, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qid=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} prayed for victory against {{ill|Abe no Sadato|en|Abe no Sadato|qid=Q3616500|lt=Abe no Sadatō|1=Abe no Sadato}} at Chōkai Gassan Shrine in {{ill|Fukura|ja|吹浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Akumi District. Upon achieving victory, Yoriyoshi built a shrine in Mogami-go, Yamagata, in the 6th year of Kōhei (康平) (1063) to enshrine divided spirits of the two gods. The shrine was venerated as a guardian deity of peace and prosperity. During the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1145058}} (1573–1591), local lord {{ill|Mogami Yoshiaki|en|Mogami Yoshiaki|qid=Q839377|lt=Mogami Yoshiaki|1=Mogami Yoshiaki}} designated {{ill|Jōjū-in Temple|ja|成就院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōjū-in Temple|1=Jōjū-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11495445}}, a Shingon sect temple, as the shrine’s administrator and donated lands to support it. The shrine gained the patronage of the {{ill|Mogami clan|en|Mogami clan|qid=Q524625|lt=Mogami clan|1=Mogami clan}}, and during the Edo period, it was revered by successive lords of the {{ill|Yamagata Domain|en|Yamagata Domain|qid=Q908769|lt=Yamagata Domain|1=Yamagata Domain}}, including the {{ill|Torii clan|en|Torii clan|qid=Q2975387|lt=Tori clan|1=Torii clan}} and the {{ill|Mizuno clan|en|Mizuno clan|qid=Q2975321|lt=Mizuno clan|1=Mizuno clan}}. The shrine was classified as a Gōsha in 1873 and was promoted to a Prefectural Shrine in 1879. In 1961, it was included as a Beppyo Shrine. == Rituals == A ritual known as {{ill|Kokudameshi|ja|穀様し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudameshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is a designated Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Yamagata City. Early on the 1st day of the 7th month in the lunar calendar, offerings of rice, cucumbers, eggplants, millet ears, and rice ears are buried in the ground. They are unearthed at the end of the 6th month the following year, and the degree of decomposition is used to predict the harvest's success. The results are printed on wooden blocks and distributed along with insect-repelling talismans to attendees. During the annual festival on August 1, mikoshi (portable shrines) representing Chōkai and Gassan are paraded through the surrounding areas by groups such as the Suishinkai. == Gallery == File:Chokai Gassan Ryosho-gu mon gate-14.jpg|Gate File:Chokai Gassan Ryosho-gu bridge.jpg|Bridge File:Chokai Gassan Ryosho-gu Inari fox statues.jpg|Inari fox statues File:Chokai Gassan Ryosho-gu inside the Shrine office.jpg|Shrine office interior File:Chokai Gassan Ryosho-gu photo-chozuya.jpg|Chōzuya (water basin) == See Also == * [[Ōmonoimi-jinja]] * [[Gassan Shrine]] * {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|qid=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai|1=Mount Chōkai}} * {{ill|Mount Gassan|en|Mount Gassan|qid=Q3174863|lt=Mount Gassan|1=Mount Gassan}} * {{ill|Three Mountains of Dewa|en|Three Mountains of Dewa|qid=Q1155726|lt=Three Mountains of Dewa|1=Three Mountains of Dewa}} == References == == Other websites == * [http://www.kankou.yamagata.yamagata.jp/db/cgi-bin/search/search.cgi?panel=detail&d01=883 Chokai Gassan Ryosho Shrine-] Yamagata City Tourism Association {{Omonoimi Faith|qq=}} {{Beppyo Shrines|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鳥海月山両所宮|version=102902671|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]] [[fr:Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]] [[ja:鳥海月山両所宮]] {{Commons category|Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gu}} [[fr:Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gu]] {{wikidata link|Q11675030}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:山形市の建築物]] [[Category:山形市の歴史]] [[Category:山形県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:山形藩]]
